mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines by more than 25 basis points over a 3-month window, signaling monetary easing. Lower discount rates reduce the present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.19% suggests potential easing room
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 2-month window, indicating labor market deterioration. This signals recession risk and triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors frontrun earnings downgrades.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.7% is near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with deteriorating labor conditions
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or mean forward return in triggered windows is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline by more than 2% month-over-month, combined with housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,100 thousand units, indicating synchronized demand destruction across consumer and residential sectors. This dual weakness predicts negative equity returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% MoM AND HOUST < 1100 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 511488M and HOUST at 1201K suggest demand resilience
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction with synchronized demand weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates by more than 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling robust supply-side momentum and pricing power. This supply expansion supports profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% MoM for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 108.48 reflects post-2017 baseline
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100 thousand net new jobs per month while unemployment rate remains stable, indicating labor market slack emerging at the margin. This soft landing signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession fears ease and the Fed pauses rate hikes.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth < 100K jobs/month AND UNRATE stable (±0.1pp)
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 149750K and UNRATE at 3.7%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion transitioning to soft landing scenario
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no incremental explanatory power beyond PAYEMS alone (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, creating negative real rates. This monetary accommodation paradox signals policy lag and equity risk-on positioning, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 252.83 and FEDFUNDS at 2.19%
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the real rate signal shows no correlation with forward returns (correlation < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling late-cycle slowdown. This growth deceleration triggers equity repricing lower over the next 5 trading days as investors reduce earnings forecasts.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 18671.50B reflects Q3 2018 data
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or mean forward return in low-growth quarters is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, indicating stagflationary pressure. This supply-side shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as profit margins compress and growth expectations fall.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.3% MoM AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.3%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 108.48 and CPIAUCSL at 252.83
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the stagflation interaction shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.20).
